methods.description: This method describes the setup and execution of experimental cremations using pig feet as human substitutes to study the effects of different conditions on cremation outcomes.

steps.description: The experimental cremations were conducted in Wallonia (Belgium) during two different sessions (October 2018 and July 2019), the first in a clearing partially sheltered from the wind and the second in a disused quarry more exposed to the wind.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct experimental cremations in different environmental conditions

steps.description: European beech (Fagus sylvatica) wood was selected to build all eight experimental pyres.
steps.searchable_summary: select and prepare wood for pyres

steps.description: Vegetable tanned cow and goat leathers were purchased to make the experimental footwear.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare experimental footwear using cow and goat leathers

steps.description: Domestic pigs were used as human substitutes, and their feet were placed in the pyres.
steps.searchable_summary: use pig feet as human substitutes in cremation experiments

steps.description: Dry farmyard manure pellets were added to one of the pyres to create an ammonia-rich combustion atmosphere.
steps.searchable_summary: add manure pellets to pyre for ammonia-rich atmosphere

methods.description: This method involves the pre-treatment of materials and the analysis of stable isotopes to study the isotopic composition of organic and inorganic components.

steps.description: The metacarpals/metatarsals from unburnt pig feet were isolated by dissection, and the periosteum was removed with a scalpel.
steps.searchable_summary: dissect and clean pig bones for analysis

steps.description: Bones were cleaned using a tungsten carbide drill bit to retain only the cortical part.
steps.searchable_summary: clean bones to retain cortical part

steps.description: Bones were then ground into powder using a countertop blender.
Carbon and nitrogen abundances and isotope compositions for organic samples were measured using a Eurovector Elemental Analyzer coupled with a Nu Perspective Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure carbon and nitrogen isotopes using EA-IRMS

methods.description: This method describes the preparation and analysis of burnt bones using ATR-FTIR spectroscopy to assess bioapatite changes.

steps.description: Sample preparation and infrared analysis of calcined bone were carried out according to Kontopoulos et al.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare calcined bone samples for ATR-FTIR analysis

steps.description: ATR-FTIR measurements on bone samples were performed using a Bruker Vertex 70v FTIR spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ATR-FTIR measurements on bone samples
